rulings.Case Title: CARLOS BARRIOS VS. HUGO LEMUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Enter Judgment Based On Default Of Stipulated Settlment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CARLOS BARRIOS Motion To Enter Judgment Based On Default Of Stipulated Settlment. Denied. The portion of the stipulation for settlement calling for judgment to be entered in the amount of $250,000 if any or all of payments totaling $57,000 are not paid is an unenforceable penalty. (Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal. App. 4th 495; see also Jade Fashion & Co., Inc. v. Harkham Industries, Inc. (2014) 229 Cal. App. 4th 635, 645-651 (explaining the rule in the Greentree case)). If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F RYDER VS. SATEL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM RYDER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Evidence. Denied. There are triable issues as to both the principal amount and the interest rate of the promissory note of January 7, 2003 as referenced in both the Operating Agreement and Addendum No. 1 to the Operating Agreement. The promissory note of January 7, 2003 is not part of the evidentiary record on this motion and thus the terms of that note must be ascertained, if at all, by other evidence. While the Agreement for Sale of Business signed May 24, 2005 has been presented as evidence of the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, that agreement does not state that it is re-stating the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, nor is that agreement referenced in either the Operating Agreement or Addendum No. 1 to the Operating Agreement, even though the Agreement for Sale of Business predates the Operating Agreement. Neither the Operating Agreement or Addendum No. 1 states the interest rate of the January 7, 2003 note, so parol evidence is permissible on that issue, and defendants have presented admissible evidence that the 4% interest rate 4% as claimed by plaintiff is disputed. Since the 4% rate is central to plaintiff's calculations of the amounts owed to him by defendants and sought by this motion, the triable issue as to the interest rate alone is sufficient to deny the motion. More fundamentally though, liberally construing defendants' evidence, that evidence shows that there was a mistake in documenting the terms of the January 7, 2003 note, even assuming that that note stated terms of $1,000,000 principal and 4% interest, which has not been shown. The parole evidence rule, as codified in this state, does not preclude evidence to show that the terms of an integrated written contract. (CCP 1856(e)). Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Ms. Stameroff are overruled. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395. Denied without prejudice to being heard by the trial judge. All three cases will be given the same trial date. Unless all parties agree to a different date, the trial date in case 526395 will be continued to April 18, 2016, the same date as the trial date in cases 526396 and 531045. The request to stay the two earlier filed cases pending the conclusion of 531045 was not properly made by way of a motion or cross-motion and has not been considered by the court. The motion to consolidate is denied on the grounds that: 1) moving parties did not show any change in circumstances since the denial of the earlier motion to consolidate and 2) moving parties failed to provide any evidence to support their assertions that essentially the same products are involved in all three cases. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SAMUIL RAITER VS. MILLA VAYNTRUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery And Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Samuil Raiter To Discovery Requests Of Defendant Milla Vayntrub
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 15, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT MILLA VAYNTRUB'S Motion To Reopen Discovery And Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Samuil Raiter To Discovery Requests Of Defendant Milla Vayntrub. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Reopen discovery is GRANTED. Defendant's Motion to compel further responses is denied as untimely. CCP Section 2030.250(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD CARMIGNANI VS. SHAUNA LEA MATLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT SHAUNA MATLIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant's moving papers satisfied her initial burden of production by showing that plaintiff's discovery responses were devoid of any factual information showing either breach of fiduciary duty or damages. Plaintiff's opposition were filed less than 14 days preceding the hearing and no court order was sought or obtained permitting plaintiff to file late papers, nor has any good cause been shown why plaintiff filed late papers. Thus, per the mandatory provision of Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(b)(2), plaintiff's late-filed papers were not considered. (Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal. App. 4th 614, 623). If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. AMBER D. GRAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Discovery-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendant Amber D Gray
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday April 15, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendant Amber D Gray. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendant Amber D Gray is DENIED without prejudice. The original or copies of all evidence that will be presented to the court at the motion hearing must be served along with the notice of motion and points and authorities. CCP § 1005(b). Here, plaintiff has not provided any evidence to show that the RFAS were served (such as a declaration) or copies of the RFAs served (with a declaration that no responses were served). The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BINH TRAN ET AL VS. LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER CU, JOSEPH CU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrers of Alexander Cu and Joseph Cu are sustained as to all three causes of action with ten days leave to amend and their motion to strike is off calendar as moot. The complaint fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts as to either Alexander Cu or Joseph Cu sufficient to state a claim for negligence or violations of Penal Code section 245.6 or Civil Code 52.1. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they are able to do so, to allege ultimate facts showing that each of these defendants, as distinct from conclusory allegations lumping all defendants together, acted negligently toward the decedent (first cause of action), participated in the illegal hazing of the decedent (second cause of action), and acted violently or threatened to act violently toward the decedent to interfere with his constitutionally protected rights (third cause of action). If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. IGLESIA NI CRISTO (CHURCH OF CHRIST), A HAWAII ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discharge Of Stakeholder And Request For A Ward Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY, Motion For Discharge Of Stakeholder And Request For A Ward Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities. Granted. The discharge is limited to the funds that were deposited with the court and does not in any way adjudicate any pending or future claims against Chicago Title for alleged breach of contract or failure to properly handle the escrow other than a claim asserting that the funds deposited with the court should have been paid to one or more of the defendants in this action per the parties' escrow contract. Plaintiff is awarded fees and costs of $18,654.75. Iglesia Ni Cristo's "defense" based on forum non conveniens lacks merit both procedurally and substantively. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM J MICHELS PHD VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 15, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrers of the Regents and Dr. Bickler are sustained as to all three causes of action with ten days leave to amend. As currently pled, all three of the causes of action are barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and the first cause of action for premises liability fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to obtain relief against the Regents under Government Code section 835 or Dr. Bickler under Government Code 840.2. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he is able to do, to allege ultimate facts showing that his claims are not barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and that he is entitled to relief against the Regents under Government Code section 835 and Dr. Bickler under Government Code 840.2. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
